questions.question: Why is training loss alone an insufficient measure of a multimodal model's quality?
options: Training loss measures fit on the training distribution; eval surfaces (retrieval, VQA, captioning) measure whether the model generalizes to held-out tasks
questions.explanation: Loss minimization is necessary but not sufficient. Held-out metrics on three task surfaces measure what the model can actually do.

questions.question: What is R@5 measuring in a 100-sample retrieval eval?
options: The fifth caption is always correct
Fraction of queries whose correct match lands in the top 5 retrieved candidates out of 100
questions.explanation: R@K = fraction of queries with the target in the top K ranked candidates. Higher is better.

questions.question: Why does BLEU-4 take the geometric mean of 1- to 4-gram precisions instead of the arithmetic mean?
options: Geometric mean is zero if any n-gram precision is zero, which is the strict matching property BLEU wants; smoothing handles the zero case
questions.explanation: The geometric mean enforces that ALL n-gram orders contribute; a 4-gram precision of zero collapses the score before smoothing.

questions.question: What does the brevity penalty in BLEU-4 protect against?
options: Short captions gaming high precision by emitting only safe high-frequency tokens; BP < 1 when generated < reference length
questions.explanation: Without BP a one-word caption matching one reference word would score 1.0. BP shrinks the score for too-short generations.

questions.question: Why does multi-reference BLEU usually score higher than single-reference BLEU on the same generation?
options: Each n-gram in the generation can match any reference, so the clipped count is higher when more references are available
questions.explanation: More references give the n-gram more chances to be matched; clipped counts grow and the precision rises.

questions.question: Which eval surface uses no model embeddings and only id-level matching?
options: VQA exact match: a predicted answer id is compared directly to a reference id with no embedding step
questions.explanation: VQA exact match is an integer comparison after the model emits its answer token; no embedding or similarity math involved.
